title: Mellow Tangy Mexican Fennel Barbacoa
description: A comforting and tangy Mexican dish featuring fennel, with a flexitarian twist, inspired by Jalisco-style barbacoa, and elevated with dashi extract for added depth of flavor.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced fennel and cook until it starts to caramelize, about 10 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the dashi extract, lime juice, and dried oregano.
Pour the dashi mixture over the fennel and garlic, and stir to combine.
Add the dried bay leaves and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Cover the Dutch oven and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the barbacoa for 2 hours, or until the fennel is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Warm the tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the barbacoa by spooning the fennel mixture onto a tortilla, topping with crumbly queso fresco, and garnishing with chopped cilantro and sliced jalapeno peppers.
